job.title: Senior Staff Accountant
job.description:
4 Corner Resources is seeking a Senior Staff Accountant to support a boutique law firm specializing in medical malpractice and healthcare-related litigation. This role combines accounting, reporting, and financial analysis while working closely with leadership to support day-to-day operations and business decision-making.

The ideal candidate is a senior-level accountant or bookkeeper who is highly organized, confident, and able to work independently. This person should be comfortable managing a high volume of transactions, keeping track of outstanding items, meeting deadlines, and taking ownership of routine accounting responsibilities with minimal supervision.

## Senior Staff Accountant Responsibilities

- Assist with month-end close (cash basis), including closed case write-offs
- Receive, verify, and enter vendor invoices into accounting and case management systems
- Prepare and process weekly check runs
- Process client credit card transactions
- Manage payroll and PTO tracking through ADP Workforce Now
- Support case-related accounting, including settlement disbursements and trust-related processes
- Create closing statements for case settlements
- Prepare cost reports for mediations and lien negotiations
- Draft correspondence related to trust disbursements
- Maintain client files tied to case settlements
- Process annual 1099s and support tax package preparation for CPA
- Analyze financial data in QuickBooks Online and extract insights from case management systems (Filevine)
- Build reports to support operational and strategic decision-making
- Track and resolve outstanding accounting items
- Manage a high volume of transactions across operating accounts, cost accounts, and corporate credit cards
- Identify opportunities to improve processes and efficiency

## Senior Staff Accountant Qualifications

- Bachelor's degree in Accounting, Finance, or related field required
- 7–10 years of accounting experience
- Proven experience with QuickBooks Online required
- Experience managing high transaction volumes and multiple priorities
- Ability to work independently with limited supervision
- Strong organizational, problem-solving, and follow-up skills
- Experience in a professional services environment preferred (law firm, CPA firm, or similar)
- Strong data analysis skills
- Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Excel and Office
- Excellent attention to detail and communication skills

## Preferred Qualifications

- Experience with legal or trust accounting
- Familiarity with Filevine or similar case management systems
- Exposure to business intelligence or financial analysis
- Interest in automation, AI tools, or process improvement
- Experience with ADP Workforce Now

## Additional Details

- Location: Maitland, FL
- Fully onsite Monday-Friday, with potential for 1 remote day per week after 90 days
- Compensation: $75,000 annually plus bonus eligibility twice per year
- Direct hire opportunity
- Benefits include health, dental, vision, 401(k) contribution, life insurance, long-term disability, paid holidays, and PTO

Apply through this board and our recruiting team will be in touch.

job.title: Bilingual Customer Experience Evaluator
job.description:
Position Summary:
We are seeking two detail-oriented and discreet Bilingual Customer Experience Evaluators to evaluate the accuracy and compliance of enrollment activities. This role involves using approved scenarios and disguises to audit representatives in the field and over the phone, ensuring they align with current regulatory and internal standards. Your feedback will help drive improvements in service quality and compliance.

Requirements:

- High School Diploma required.
- 1 Candidate must be bilingual in English and Spanish.
- 1 Candidate must be English and Mandarin, Cantonese, or Fukienese.
- Strong proficiency in Microsoft Excel for reporting and documentation.
- Excellent attention to detail, verbal and written communication, and time management skills.
- Ability to create and wear disguises (e.g., wigs, hats, glasses) to ensure anonymity during audits.

Technical Skills:

- Comfortable using Microsoft Office (Excel, Word, Outlook), iPhone apps, and web-based tools (e.g., Google Maps).
- Ability to accurately type, copy/paste, highlight, and format in Word and Excel.

Key Responsibilities:

- Conduct field and phone audits using scripted scenarios while maintaining an anonymous identity.
- Record and document encounters; transcribe interactions where applicable and flag any non-compliance or misinformation.
- Monitor sales representatives for adherence to company guidelines and federal/state regulations.
- Submit daily and weekly audit summaries using Excel-based templates.

Apply through this board and our recruiting team will be in touch.

job.title: Recruiter
job.description:
4 Corner Resources is looking for a recruiter to join our team. This is a full-cycle role on our internal recruiting staff: you'll source candidates, run phone and Zoom screens, check references, prep resumes for client submission, and build relationships that turn into placements. The role is remote-first with occasional in-person meetings in Orlando or Tampa, so you'll need to be local to Central Florida.

Florida residency required.

Compensation starts at $45,000 plus bonus and commission.

Responsibilities

- Source and screen candidates by phone, Zoom, and in person
- Conduct interviews and assess candidate quality, motivation, and fit
- Build and maintain candidate profiles in the recruiting database
- Check manager-level references and complete pre-employment steps (background checks, drug tests, skill assessments)
- Edit and prepare resumes and candidate write-ups for client submission
- Negotiate terms of employment and manage post-hire communications
- Obtain referrals, set prospective client meetings, and support client needs when account managers are unavailable
- Build lasting relationships with candidates, contractors, and referral sources
- Stay current on local market conditions and attend networking and professional association events

Qualifications

- Located in Central Florida (Orlando or Tampa area) with availability for occasional onsite meetings
- Strong communication skills and the ability to build rapport by phone and in person
- Comfort asking direct, sometimes difficult questions
- Punctual, organized, detail-oriented, with a strong sense of urgency

Preferred Qualifications

- Around one year of recruiting, staffing, sales, or related customer-facing experience
- Interest in building a long-term career in recruiting

Apply through this board and our recruiting team will be in touch.

job.title: IT Business Analyst
job.description:
4 Corner Resources is seeking an IT Business Analyst for a fast-paced, project-driven enterprise IT environment. This role is ideal for a Business Analyst who thrives in Agile environments, enjoys collaborating across technical and business teams, and can drive projects from requirements gathering through deployment and support.

What you do:

- Gather and translate business requirements into user stories, use cases, and functional specs
- Serve as the liaison between business stakeholders and the IT delivery team
- Participate in daily Scrum ceremonies and manage backlog grooming and sprint planning
- Write and present change requests at the Change Advisory Board
- Coordinate release communications and implementation plans
- Support UAT, hypercare, and root cause analysis post go-live
- Step into a Product Owner role when there is no dedicated PO on the project

What you bring:

- Proven BA experience in an IT environment
- Strong knowledge of Agile and Scrum methodology
- Hands-on experience with Jira, ADO, or similar tools
- Experience with CAB processes and ITSM frameworks
- Ability to write detailed functional documentation
- Strong stakeholder management and facilitation skills

Additional Details:

- Location: Orlando, FL
- Length: 6-12 month contract (potential for conversion)
- Schedule: Monday-Friday 8:00AM-5:00PM

Apply through this board and our recruiting team will be in touch.

job.title: Field Service Manager
job.description:
Location: On-site in York, Pennsylvania

Pay Rate: $90,000 – $171,000 per year depending on experience. Seniority will significantly affect compensation for this role.

Employment Type: Full-Time, Direct Hire

Introduction

Our client is seeking a Manager of Field Services, Site Management to lead the planning and execution of field site activities for hydroelectric projects. This position is responsible for ensuring projects are completed safely, on schedule, within budget, and according to established quality requirements.

The Manager will oversee site management personnel, establish field execution procedures, support project staffing, monitor field performance, and provide leadership to Site Managers and Supervisors.

Responsibilities

- Oversee the execution of field service activities across assigned projects.
- Develop and maintain standard processes, procedures, and tools for planning and executing field site activities.
- Ensure field operations comply with technical requirements and applicable legal and regional regulations.
- Identify, hire, assign, coach, and manage Site Managers and Supervisors.
- Ensure project sites are properly staffed according to project execution plans.
- Oversee timekeeping and compensation processes for field labor and supervisory personnel.
- Establish qualification requirements and coordinate necessary training initiatives.
- Monitor and improve safety, quality, and overall performance at project sites.
- Support field activity planning and the implementation of standardized planning systems and tools.
- Ensure work procedures and quality plans are available and followed.
- Monitor project costs, identify potential risks, and implement corrective actions.
- Promote best practices and lessons learned across field sites.
- Support the implementation of site layouts, infrastructure, tooling, and fixtures.
- Oversee site purchasing and sourcing activities.

Requirements

- High school diploma or GED.

- Previous experience in a supervisory or management position.

- Previous experience as a Site Manager or Supervisor on hydro turbine or generator projects.

- Ability to read and interpret blueprints, specifications, project schedules, and budgets.

- Strong communication, leadership, and interpersonal skills.

- Proficiency with Microsoft Office.

- Ability to travel occasionally to field sites.

- Ability to work in both office and active field site environments.

- Ability to stand and walk for extended periods and occasionally lift, reach, stoop, or crouch.

- Ability to work safely in field environments that may have elevated noise levels.

Apply through this board and our recruiting team will be in touch.

job.title: Project Engineer
job.description:
Location: On-site in Neenah, Wisconsin

Pay Rate: $70,000 – $100,000 / year

Employment Type: Full-Time, Direct Hire

Introduction

Our client is seeking an Engineer II, Projects to manage customer projects within a roll shop environment. This position will provide engineering, technical, commercial, and project management support from the initial quotation through project completion.

Responsibilities

- Manage customer projects from initial quotation through completion.

- Communicate with customers regarding engineering designs, project requirements, pricing, questions, and project progress.

- Prepare project quotations, supporting documentation, preliminary design proposals, and scopes of work for mechanical services, modifications, repairs, and testing.

- Review customer drawings, specifications, and related documentation to determine project requirements and component feasibility.

- Serve as a liaison between engineering and mechanical service operations.

- Work with mechanical personnel to resolve manufacturing and design issues.

- Coordinate with purchasing, quality control, sales, production, accounting, metal finishing, assembly, vendors, and outside sales representatives.

- Generate and update process documentation, work instructions, flowcharts, and related engineering documents.

- Participate in engineering and general design reviews.

- Identify and support cost-reduction and process-improvement opportunities.

- Assist with root-cause investigations and corrective actions.

- Generate and track KPI data and corrective action progress.

- Support safety investigations, safety initiatives, Lean practices, and Operational Excellence programs.

- Manage multiple projects and priorities while meeting customer and business deadlines.

Requirements

- Bachelor's degree in engineering from an accredited four-year college or university. Relevant professional experience may be considered in place of a college degree.

- Proficiency with Microsoft Office applications, ERP systems, and electronic document control systems.

- Strong verbal and written communication skills.

- Ability to communicate effectively with engineers, customers, vendors, and shop-floor team members.

- Strong project management, troubleshooting, analytical, and problem-solving skills.

- Ability to independently manage multiple projects, responsibilities, and deadlines.

- Ability to prioritize responsibilities and make sound decisions in a deadline-driven environment.

- Professional, personable, and diplomatic communication style.

- Self-motivated and capable of working independently with limited supervision.

- Ability to teach, coach, and support other team members.

Preferred Qualifications

- Experience working within a manufacturing environment.

- Previous project management experience.

- Knowledge of paper machine rolls, paper manufacturing equipment, or paper mill operations.

- Experience reviewing engineering drawings, specifications, and manufacturing requirements.

- Experience supporting mechanical repair, modification, or testing projects.

- Familiarity with Lean manufacturing and Operational Excellence principles.

Physical Requirements

- Ability to stand for extended periods, walk, and reach with hands and arms.

- Ability to lift and move up to 50 pounds.

- Ability to perform work requiring close vision, distance vision, color vision, depth perception, and the ability to adjust focus.

- Ability to work within indoor office and manufacturing shop environments.

- May be exposed to noise, welding activities, grinding dust, and fumes within shop areas.

- Ability to travel up to 10% as business needs require.

This job description is intended to provide a general overview of the position and is not an all-inclusive list of responsibilities, requirements, or working conditions.

Apply through this board and our recruiting team will be in touch.

job.title: Mechanical Engineer II
job.description:
Location: On-site in York, Pennsylvania
Pay Rate: Up to $110,000 / year
Employment Type: Full-Time, Direct Hire

Introduction

Our client is seeking an Engineer 2 to develop engineering design solutions from project inception through production. This position will work with reduced supervision and guidance from experienced engineering professionals to solve routine design problems, support customer projects, and contribute to process improvement and standardization initiatives.

The Engineer 2 may serve as the lead engineer for smaller projects and perform component engineering for rehabilitation projects and new components. This role will support projects from the proposal stage through manufacturing, installation, testing, and project completion.

Responsibilities

- Prepare and update engineering designs for customer projects.

- Provide technical project leadership from contract award through project completion.

- Support proposals by preparing preliminary engineering designs and technical solutions.

- Define sourcing and manufacturing strategies beginning during the proposal stage.

- Create 3D solid models of components and assemblies.

- Prepare 2D manufacturing drawings independently or in collaboration with designers.

- Operate CAD and CAE software to complete engineering assignments.

- Perform required design calculations using standard engineering principles, hand calculations, and proprietary design tools.

- Review and release engineering documents and bills of materials in consultation with experienced engineers.

- Locate and evaluate OEM drawings from internal archives and customer-provided drawing files.

- Use SAP to create bills of materials that support manufacturing, sourcing, logistics, and installation requirements.

- Participate in and lead engineering design reviews.

- Prepare design revisions and process engineering change notifications.

- Provide dispositions for nonconforming materials, manufactured components, assemblies, and installed or tested equipment.

- Assist with the development of engineering schedules for assigned projects.

- Communicate project progress, milestones, workload, challenges, and potential delays to supervisors.

- Collaborate with manufacturing, sourcing, logistics, vendors, and field service teams to develop cost-effective design and installation solutions.

- Support quality teams with continuous improvement and problem-solving initiatives.

- Assist test engineers during equipment startup and commissioning.

- Evaluate test results and verify equipment performance against guaranteed values.

- Prepare technical reports, forms, and project documentation.

- Communicate with customers regarding engineering design questions and concerns.

- Contribute to engineering process improvement and standardization initiatives.

Required Qualifications

- Bachelor's degree in Engineering.

- Three to five years of engineering experience with a demonstrated record of successful project execution.

- Custom engineering design experience involving wind turbines, turbine generators, gas turbines, or related equipment within the energy sector.

- Experience developing engineering designs, calculations, drawings, and technical documentation.

- Ability to create or review 3D models, 2D drawings, and bills of materials.

- Strong teamwork and collaboration skills.

- Excellent written and verbal communication skills.

- Ability to manage multiple priorities based on changing business needs.

- Strong organizational and time-management skills.

Preferred Qualifications

- Experience supporting industrial equipment, rehabilitation projects, or engineered-to-order products.

- Experience using CAD or CAE design software.

- Experience creating bills of materials in SAP.

- Experience supporting manufacturing, sourcing, installation, testing, or commissioning activities.

- Experience providing technical leadership for smaller engineering projects.

Apply through this board and our recruiting team will be in touch.

job.title: Outpatient Quality Improvement Consultant
job.description:
Our client is seeking an Outpatient Quality Improvement Consultant to support physician practices, outpatient clinics, and ambulatory healthcare organizations in improving patient outcomes, preventive care performance, and operational efficiency across multiple states.

This role partners with providers, practice leadership, and interdisciplinary teams to evaluate workflows, analyze quality metrics, identify care gaps, and support performance improvement initiatives in outpatient healthcare settings. The organization offers a collaborative, remote-first culture focused on advancing quality care delivery and population health outcomes.

### Required qualifications:

• Bachelor's degree in nursing, public health, healthcare administration, or related field
• 3+ years of experience in outpatient healthcare, physician practice operations, population health, managed care, or ambulatory quality improvement
• Experience with HEDIS, preventive care metrics, chronic disease management, or value-based care initiatives
• Familiarity with quality improvement methodologies such as root cause analysis, PDSA, and process mapping
• Experience interpreting EHR data, quality dashboards, and provider performance metrics
• Strong communication and provider relationship management skills
• Ability to work independently in a remote environment and manage multiple priorities

### Preferred backgrounds:

• Population Health Specialist
• HEDIS or Quality Improvement Coordinator
• Clinical Operations Manager
• Practice Administrator
• Managed Care Quality Specialist
• Care Management or Value-Based Care Professional
• Outpatient Quality Analyst

### Day-to-day responsibilities:

• Support outpatient providers with quality improvement and workflow optimization initiatives
• Analyze preventive care metrics, chronic disease outcomes, and quality performance data
• Identify care gaps and guide providers on documentation and operational best practices
• Assist practices with performance improvement initiatives tied to CMS and value-based care measures
• Monitor provider progress and maintain quality-related documentation within assigned systems
• Build relationships with physician practices and outpatient clinical teams across assigned territories

### Benefits:

• Comprehensive medical, dental, and vision coverage
• 401(k) with employer contribution
• Generous PTO and paid holidays
• Remote-first work environment
• Friday schedules ending at noon
• All travel expenses covered for required meetings

### Culture:

This organization is known for its collaborative and mission-driven environment. Leadership is stable, employee retention is high, and many team members describe the culture as supportive, flexible, and aligned with a shared commitment to improving healthcare. Growth paths exist within a matrix structure, allowing advancement from Associate to Consultant to Senior Consultant levels.

### Salary / Pay Range:

$78,000 – $85,000 annually

### Work Schedule:

Monday–Friday, 8:30 AM – 5:00 PM

Remote in Florida

Minimal travel outside of the two required annual meetings

Apply through this board and our recruiting team will be in touch.

job.title: Hospital Quality Improvement Consultant
job.description:
Our client is seeking a Hospital Quality Improvement Consultant to support acute care hospitals and healthcare systems in improving patient outcomes, operational performance, and regulatory compliance across multiple states.

This role works closely with hospital leadership, quality departments, clinical teams, and operational stakeholders to evaluate workflows, analyze quality data, identify performance gaps, and support process improvement initiatives. The organization offers a collaborative, mission-driven, remote-first environment focused on advancing healthcare quality at scale.

### Required qualifications:

• Bachelor's degree in nursing, public health, healthcare administration, or related field
• 3+ years of experience in hospital quality, patient safety, clinical operations, or acute care environments
• Experience with hospital quality metrics, regulatory compliance, or accreditation standards
• Familiarity with root cause analysis, process mapping, PDSA, Lean, Six Sigma, or similar methodologies
• Ability to analyze EHR data, quality dashboards, and performance metrics
• Strong communication and collaboration skills across interdisciplinary teams
• Ability to work independently in a remote environment and manage multiple priorities

### Preferred backgrounds:

• Hospital Quality Coordinator
• Patient Safety Specialist
• Clinical Quality Manager
• Performance Improvement Specialist
• Infection Preventionist
• Risk Management or Accreditation Leader
• Lean/Six Sigma Professional

### Day-to-day responsibilities:

• Analyze hospital quality metrics, workflows, and operational performance data
• Support initiatives related to readmissions, patient safety, throughput, infection prevention, and clinical outcomes
• Assist hospitals with regulatory readiness and accreditation support
• Guide providers on process improvement initiatives and best practices
• Monitor performance trends and support ongoing quality improvement activities
• Collaborate with clinical and operational teams across multiple provider organizations

### Benefits:

• Comprehensive medical, dental, and vision coverage
• 401(k) with employer contribution
• Generous PTO and paid holidays
• Remote-first work environment
• Friday schedules ending at noon
• All travel expenses covered for required meetings

### Culture:

This organization is known for its collaborative and mission-driven environment. Leadership is stable, employee retention is high, and many team members describe the culture as supportive, flexible, and aligned with a shared commitment to improving healthcare. Growth paths exist within a matrix structure, allowing advancement from Associate to Consultant to Senior Consultant levels.

### Salary / Pay Range:

$78,000 – $85,000 annually

### Work Schedule:

Monday–Friday, 8:30 AM – 5:00 PM

Remote role requiring residency within designated states: Alabama, Florida, Georgia, Kentucky, Louisiana, Mississippi, North Carolina, South Carolina, Tennessee, Virginia, Arkansas, and West Virginia

Minimal travel outside of the two required annual meetings

Apply through this board and our recruiting team will be in touch.

job.title: Nursing Home Quality Improvement Consultant
job.description:
Our client is seeking a Nursing Home Quality Improvement Consultant to support Skilled Nursing Facilities and Long Term Care providers in improving patient outcomes, strengthening regulatory performance, and enhancing quality measures across multiple states.

This role partners directly with Nursing Home Administrators, Directors of Nursing, MDS leaders, and interdisciplinary teams to analyze quality data, identify operational gaps, support QAPI initiatives, and guide performance improvement efforts. The organization offers a highly collaborative, remote-first environment focused on improving care delivery within post acute and long term care settings.

### Required qualifications:

• Bachelor's degree in nursing, public health, healthcare administration, or related field
• 3+ years of experience within Skilled Nursing Facilities or nursing home
• Must have experience working as a Licensed Nursing Home Administrator (LNHA) or Director of Nursing (DON)
• Experience with QAPI, CMS quality measures, Five-Star Ratings, survey readiness, or regulatory compliance
• Familiarity with root cause analysis, process mapping, PDSA, and quality improvement methodologies
• Experience interpreting MDS, EHR, or quality performance data
• Strong communication and relationship-building skills with nursing home leadership teams
• Ability to work independently in a remote environment and manage multiple priorities

### Preferred backgrounds:

• Director of Nursing (DON)
• Assistant Director of Nursing (ADON)
• Nursing Home Administrator (NHA/LNHA)

### Day-to-day responsibilities:

• Support nursing homes with quality improvement initiatives and regulatory performance
• Analyze quality metrics related to falls, infections, rehospitalizations, weight loss, antipsychotic use, and CMS measures
• Guide facilities through QAPI initiatives and process improvement strategies
• Assist providers with survey readiness, Plans of Correction, and operational improvement efforts
• Interpret MDS and EHR data to identify trends and opportunities for improvement
• Maintain ongoing communication with facility leadership teams and assigned provider groups

### Benefits:

• Comprehensive medical, dental, and vision coverage
• 401(k) with employer contribution
• Generous PTO and paid holidays
• Remote-first work environment
• Friday schedules ending at noon
• All travel expenses covered for required meetings

### Culture:

This organization is known for its collaborative and mission-driven environment. Leadership is stable, employee retention is high, and many team members describe the culture as supportive, flexible, and aligned with a shared commitment to improving healthcare. Growth paths exist within a matrix structure, allowing advancement from Associate to Consultant to Senior Consultant levels.

### Salary / Pay Range:

$78,000 – $85,000 annually

### Work Schedule:

Monday–Friday, 8:30 AM – 5:00 PM

Remote role requiring residency within designated states: Alabama, Florida, Georgia, Kentucky, Louisiana, Mississippi, North Carolina, South Carolina, Tennessee, Virginia, Arkansas, and West Virginia

Minimal travel outside of the two required annual meetings

Apply through this board and our recruiting team will be in touch.

job.title: Contract Manager
job.description:
Location: On-site in York, Pennsylvania

Pay Rate: 112,000 – $143,000 /year

Employment Type: Full-Time

Introduction

Our client is seeking a Contract Manager to oversee contractual aspects of assigned projects from the offer stage through successful contract completion. This role will focus on contract review, risk identification, risk mitigation, claim management, change management, and commercial project support.

The Contract Manager will partner with Offer Project Managers, Project Managers, legal, insurance, finance, tax, and other internal teams to ensure contractual requirements are understood, documented, managed, and executed accurately throughout the project lifecycle.

Responsibilities

- Take ownership of contractual requirements for assigned projects.

- Serve as the business partner for Offer Project Managers during the offer phase and Project Managers during the execution phase.

- Review and analyze contracts to identify risks, mitigation strategies, and project management requirements.

- Establish project-specific commercial risk mitigation plans in accordance with the risk management process.

- Consult with project team members to support implementation of risk mitigation plans.

- Coordinate with legal, insurance, finance, tax, and other corporate departments to mitigate financial and market risk exposure.

- Develop and implement processes to administer, document, accept, challenge, and defend incoming and outgoing change requests.

- Manage the contract change and claim process to ensure deviations from contract terms are properly captured and processed.

- Manage required contractual securities, including bonds, letters of credit, parent company guarantees, and insurance policies.

- Support positive cash flow during project execution.

- Manage commercial close-out and post-completion processes, including warranty periods and warranty claims.

- Work across multiple priorities while maintaining accuracy, organization, and strong internal and external relationships.

Required Qualifications

- Bachelor's degree in Business Administration, legal studies, or a related field.

- 5+ years of experience in contract management, risk management, or a related area.

- Experience reviewing, analyzing, and managing contract terms, risks, changes, and claims.

- Ability to work through complex business scenarios and different contract structures.

- Strong analytical and cross-functional collaboration skills.

- Excellent written and verbal communication skills.

- Proficiency with Microsoft Office Suite or related software.

- Strong organizational skills with the ability to manage multiple priorities accurately.

Preferred Qualifications

- Experience supporting commercial management, project management, construction, infrastructure, manufacturing, industrial equipment, or related project-based environments.

- Experience working with legal, insurance, finance, tax, or other corporate support teams.

- Experience managing contractual securities, insurance requirements, warranty claims, or commercial project close-out processes.

Company Benefits & Culture

This is a full-time opportunity with the client. The role offers the opportunity to support complex project-based contract management work while partnering closely with project management, commercial management, legal, finance, insurance, tax, and customer-facing teams.

Candidates who do not meet every single requirement but believe they would be a strong fit are encouraged to apply.

Apply through this board and our recruiting team will be in touch.

job.title: Financial Controller
job.description:
Location: Onsite in West Monroe, Louisiana
Pay Rate: $110,000 – $135,000 + bonus
Employment Type: Full-Time, Direct Hire

Introduction

Our client is seeking a Financial Controller II to serve as a business partner for operations and help drive financial performance across two manufacturing sites. This role combines plant controlling, financial controlling, FP&A, financial reporting, and leadership responsibilities.

The Financial Controller II will oversee financial reporting for the West Monroe, LA and Austell, GA manufacturing sites, including balance sheet, P&L, cash flow, and key business metrics. This person will also identify opportunities to improve financial performance, processes, reporting tools, and systems.

Responsibilities

- Oversee accurate and timely preparation of financial statements in accordance with IFRS.
- Lead month-end and year-end close processes, including coordination with internal and external auditors.
- Manage fixed assets, including capitalization, depreciation, and disposals.
- Ensure compliance with corporate policies, regulatory requirements, and treasury guidelines.
- Maintain a strong internal control environment and serve as Compliance Officer for supported units.
- Drive budgeting, forecasting, and long-range planning in partnership with operational and commercial leaders.
- Analyze cost structures, standard costs, and manufacturing variances.
- Conduct product profitability and margin analysis to identify cost optimization opportunities.
- Review financial performance and proactively identify operational and financial improvement opportunities.
- Provide clear financial insights to local leadership teams.
- Evaluate and improve financial processes, reporting tools, and systems to drive business efficiency.
- Lead, coach, and develop Finance and Controlling professionals.

Required Qualifications

- Bachelor's degree in Finance, Economics, Business Administration, or related field.
- 5+ years of experience in controlling, financial management, FP&A, or a related finance function.
- Experience with financial statements, cost controlling, cost accounting, and product costing.
- Experience supporting budgeting, forecasting, and financial planning.
- Strong critical thinking, problem-solving, and business partnership skills.
- Experience working with ERP systems.
- Proficiency with Microsoft Office, especially Excel and PowerPoint.
- Strong communication, interpersonal, and organizational skills.
- Ability to work onsite in West Monroe, Louisiana.
- Ability to travel occasionally to another supported site, up to 25%.

Preferred Qualifications

- Finance or controlling experience in an industrial or manufacturing environment.
- Plant Controller, Financial Controller, or similar background.
- Experience with JD Edwards.
- SAP experience.
- Previous leadership experience.
- Strong understanding of IFRS.

Company Benefits & Culture

- Full benefits package including major medical, 401k with match, and PTO.
- Direct hire opportunity with bonus potential.
- Opportunity to serve as a finance business partner for manufacturing operations.
- Leadership role with responsibility for developing finance professionals.
- Role focused on process improvement, financial performance, and operational partnership.

Apply through this board and our recruiting team will be in touch.

job.title: Enrollment & Billing Specialist
job.description:
6 Month Contract
Location: Lake Mary, FL (Hybrid)
Schedule: Monday–Friday, 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM
3 days onsite (Tuesday–Thursday)
Pay Rate: $28/hour

4 Corner Resources is seeking an experienced Enrollment & Billing Specialist to support healthcare enrollment, eligibility, billing, and member account operations across multiple lines of business, including Medicare and Medicaid plans. This role requires strong attention to detail, excellent data entry accuracy, advanced Excel skills, and the ability to work in a fast-paced, team-oriented environment.

Key Responsibilities

- Process member enrollments, disenrollments, terminations, and account updates accurately and within established service level agreements
- Review and reconcile eligibility and billing reports to ensure member records and premium payments are correctly applied
- Perform high-volume data entry and maintain accuracy across multiple systems and platforms
- Investigate and resolve member account discrepancies, billing issues, and eligibility concerns
- Process payments, refunds, and account adjustments as needed
- Review and update transactions within customer management and workflow systems
- Collaborate with Contact Center Operations, Member Services, and cross-functional teams to resolve escalated member inquiries
- Working within vendor and healthcare platforms to validate eligibility and enrollment information
- Maintain daily productivity and quality standards
- Support additional operational and administrative duties as assigned

Required Qualifications

- High school diploma or GED required
- Experience working in healthcare enrollment, billing, eligibility, or member services operations
- Strong data entry and multitasking skills in high-volume environments
- Advanced Microsoft Excel experience, including pivot tables, filtering, and reconciliation reporting
- Proficiency with Microsoft Outlook and Word
- Experience using CRM, workflow management, or healthcare processing systems
- Strong attention to detail and organizational skills
- Ability to work collaboratively within a team environment
- Strong verbal and written communication skills
- Ability to work overtime as needed

Preferred Qualifications

- Bachelor's degree preferred
- Previous experience in Medicare, Medicaid, managed care, or commercial insurance environments
- Experience with auditing, reconciliation, or analytical reporting
- Experience working with eligibility platforms and vendor systems
- Knowledge of healthcare enrollment workflows and member record processing
- Experience with Microsoft Access or data management systems preferred

Apply through this board and our recruiting team will be in touch.

job.title: Field Service Spares Engineer
job.description:
Location: On-site in Appleton, Wisconsin
Pay Rate: $82,000 – $100,000
Employment Type: Full-Time, Direct Hire

Introduction

Our client is seeking a Technical Spare Parts Specialist to support technical spare parts identification, design, documentation, and customer support. This role will work closely with engineering, manufacturing, sales, purchasing, quality, service teams, and customers to ensure accurate part identification, technical recommendations, and support for parts and services needs.

Responsibilities

- Identify parts from assemblies, multi-drawings, and technical documentation

- Create, adapt, and revise 2D/3D models and drawings

- Define tolerances and specifications in alignment with standards under guidance

- Support the creation of Material Masters for identified parts

- Prepare maintenance kits, BOMs, and technical recommendations

- Support cost department and sales teams with technical arguments and documentation

- Conduct customer site visits for equipment inspections and technical recommendations

- Assist customers with detailed explanations of requested parts

- Communicate directly with customers regarding spare parts requests

- Apply knowledge of part life cycle and equipment processes

- Determine the best source for purchased and manufactured parts using local and international suppliers

- Support warranty investigations, vendors, and workshop teams with inputs and documentation

- Participate in continuous improvement of processes, standards, and customer satisfaction

- Travel up to 20% for customer visits and production floor work

Required Qualifications

- Bachelor's degree in Mechanical Engineering, Industrial Engineering, or a related engineering field, or proven technical experience in spare parts or a related industry

- Previous knowledge of paper machines or paper production processes

- Experience with technical drawing interpretation

- Experience with CAD software

- Strong analytical and problem-solving skills

- Hands-on experience in technical environments

- Ability to work independently and collaborate cross-functionally

- Strong communication skills with technical and non-technical audiences

Preferred Qualifications

- Previous experience in parts and services

- Paper industry experience

- Experience with SAP

- Experience with documentation systems

Company Benefits & Culture

This is a full-time, direct hire opportunity with the client. This role offers the opportunity to work in a highly visible technical position supporting spare parts, service, engineering, manufacturing, sales, purchasing, quality, and customer-facing initiatives. The position is well-suited for someone who enjoys applying technical expertise, working cross-functionally, and supporting a growing segment of the organization.

Apply through this board and our recruiting team will be in touch.

job.title: Franchise Corporate Trainer
job.description:
4 Corner Resources is seeking a Franchise Corporate Trainer on behalf of our client, a rapidly expanding franchise organization. This role is instrumental in helping franchise owners succeed through operational training, ongoing consulting, and consistent oversight that protects and strengthens the brand.

Willingness to travel across Florida and out of state approximately 85% of the time is required.

Corporate Trainer Key Responsibilities:

- Deliver structured training programs before, during, and after store openings, ensuring franchisees are fully equipped to operate independently.
- Serve as a trusted advisor to franchise owners, building strong, collaborative relationships through clear communication and hands-on support.
- Conduct regular on-site visits to evaluate performance, provide coaching, and identify opportunities for operational improvement.
- Monitor franchisee compliance with company policies, operational procedures, and brand standards to maintain consistency across all locations.
- Provide tailored consulting and problem-solving support to help franchise owners overcome challenges, improve profitability, and drive long-term business growth.
- Gather feedback from franchisees and share insights with leadership to inform future training and operational strategies.
- Maintain accurate training records, reports, and evaluations to track franchisee progress and overall program effectiveness.

Corporate Trainer Qualifications:

- Proven background in operational leadership, with Quick Service Restaurant (QSR) management experience strongly preferred.
- Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with a personable and approachable style and patience in building relationships.
- Strong business acumen, with a consultative approach to problem solving and guiding franchisees toward improved results.
- Highly motivated, proactive, and able to balance independent work with supporting multiple stakeholders.
- Demonstrated ability to manage travel, schedules, and priorities while maintaining focus on long-term goals.
- Willingness and ability to travel 85% of the time to support franchise operations across Florida and select out-of-state markets.

Corporate Trainer Additional Details:

- Full-time, direct hire position
- Flexible schedule required, including willingness to work weekends for store openings
- Primary territory covers Jacksonville, Ocala, and Lake City, FL with out-of-state travel as needed
- Salary starting at $57,500k annually, with flexibility based on experience
- Competitive compensation package including:
* Health, dental, and vision insurance
* 401(k) with company match
* Paid time off and paid holidays
* Company vehicle provided or mileage reimbursement if using personal vehicle
* Covered travel expenses
* Supportive, growth-focused work environment

Apply through this board and our recruiting team will be in touch.

job.title: Site Supervisor
job.description:
Job Title: Site Supervisor
Location: Can be located anywhere in the U.S – 95% travel required
Salary / Pay Range: $100,000 annually plus bonus
Employment Type: Full-Time

Our client is seeking a Site Supervisor to plan, prepare, and supervise the disassembly and installation of hydro power plant equipment at field sites. This role will oversee work involving hydro-turbines, generators, control equipment, and related support equipment while directing craft labor crews and subcontractors to ensure work is completed safely, accurately, on schedule, and within budget.

Responsibilities

- Direct foremen, craft labor, and subcontractors for assigned work scopes

- Supervise disassembly, maintenance, rehabilitation, and installation activities for hydro turbines, generators, and auxiliary systems

- Support safety planning and enforce site safety rules and procedures

- Perform detailed planning and preparation for assigned work scopes, including handoffs with support groups

- Work with engineering, quality, and customer teams to clarify technical procedures, drawings, and field data requirements

- Identify and mitigate safety and quality risks during planning and execution

- Ensure materials and components meet specifications and are free of defects before installation

- Plan and ensure availability of fixtures, tools, and required equipment

- Ensure quality requirements are understood, measured, inspected, and documented as required

- Escalate safety, technical, quality, or schedule issues to the Site Manager

- Support workforce capacity planning with the Site Manager

- Promote a professional and supportive work environment with craft labor and subcontractors

- Identify and document schedule and quality deviations

- Lead site incident investigations for safety and quality issues and drive corrective actions

Required Qualifications

- High school diploma required

- Minimum of 5 years of experience

- Minimum of 3 years of relevant experience in construction, hydro preferred, or journeyman trades in a leadership role

- Ability to identify and mitigate safety risks while maintaining a safe work environment for all site personnel

- Strong attention to detail and accuracy

- Excellent organizational and communication skills

- Excellent spoken and written English skills

- Ability to engage vendors and engineering personnel to define solutions that reduce cost and risk

- Ability to provide leadership and ensure compliant execution according to contractual requirements

- Ability to support project objectives related to safety, quality, schedule, and cost

- OSHA 30 certification

- Ability to meet physical requirements of the role, including use of PPE, lifting up to 50 lbs., climbing ladders and stairs, working in confined spaces, and working at heights

Preferred Qualifications

- Hydro construction experience

- General Foreman or similar field leadership experience

- Experience supervising installation, maintenance, or rehabilitation work involving turbines, generators, or auxiliary systems

Company Benefits & Culture

Our client offers the opportunity to support technical field service work in a hands-on hydro power environment. This role works closely with site management, craft labor, subcontractors, engineering, quality, and customer teams to support safe, efficient, and contract-compliant project execution.

Apply through this board and our recruiting team will be in touch.

job.title: Site Manager
job.description:
Job Title: Site Manager
Location: Can be located anywhere in the U.S – 95% travel required
Salary / Pay Range: $100,000 annually plus bonus
Employment Type: Full-Time

Our client is seeking a Site Manager to oversee site supervision and site project management for hydro power plant equipment installations, upgrades, and maintenance. This role will support hydro-turbine, generator, control equipment, and related electrical and mechanical systems while ensuring work is completed safely, on schedule, within budget, and in accordance with contractual and quality requirements.

Responsibilities

- Serve as the single point of accountability for site work planning, performance, and site employees

- Perform detailed planning for site work, including interfaces and handoffs with support groups

- Lead erection and commissioning efforts for hydro-electric equipment and supporting systems

- Manage site performance related to safety, quality, cost, schedule, and scope

- Maintain strong customer relationships through clear communication and professional site standards

- Oversee financial performance of on-site activities

- Identify and communicate additional sales opportunities with sales and project management

- Support continuous improvement by sharing best practices and lessons learned

- Work closely with project management to ensure contractual obligations are met

- Support safety planning and enforce site safety rules and procedures

- Identify and mitigate safety and quality risks during planning and execution

- Lead site teams, craft labor, and subcontractors in a professional and supportive manner

- Document schedule, quality, and scope deviations

- Lead incident investigations for safety and quality issues and drive corrective actions

Required Qualifications

- High school diploma required

- Bachelor of Engineering or equivalent relevant experience

- Minimum of 10 years of professional experience

- Minimum of 5 years of relevant experience in construction, hydro preferred, or journeyman trades in leadership roles

- Technical understanding of hydroelectric power plant equipment, rotating machinery, heavy manufacturing, or heavy construction

- Ability to manage 1 or 2 systems such as generator, turbine, E-BoP, M-BoP, steel structures, or hydraulics

- Ability to manage one area of a construction or rehabilitation project, such as powerhouse, intake, substation, dam, onsite manufacturing, or tunnel

- Ability to identify and mitigate safety risks while maintaining a safe work environment

- Understanding of contractual terms and conditions related to commercial project execution

- Experience identifying change work or claim opportunities and supporting documentation efforts

- Experience leading craft labor and professional teams

- Strong attention to detail and accuracy

- Excellent organizational and communication skills

- Strong spoken and written English skills

- Ability to engage vendors and engineering teams to define solutions that reduce cost and risk

- Ability to meet physical requirements of the role, including use of PPE, lifting up to 50 lbs., climbing ladders and stairs, working in confined spaces, and working at heights

Preferred Qualifications

- Hydro construction or hydro rehabilitation project experience

- General Foreman or similar site leadership experience

- Experience managing field work involving generator, turbine, electrical balance of plant, mechanical balance of plant, steel structures, or hydraulics

Company Benefits & Culture

Our client offers the opportunity to lead field site operations on technical hydro power projects. This role works closely with project management, field service leadership, customers, subcontractors, suppliers, craft labor, and engineering teams to support safe, efficient, and contract-compliant project execution.

Apply through this board and our recruiting team will be in touch.

job.title: Field Service Engineer
job.description:
Job Title: Field Service Engineer
Location: York, PA
Salary / Pay Range: $90,000 annually
Employment Type: Full-Time

Our client is seeking a Field Service Engineer to plan and manage the field service scope of projects while ensuring work is completed within budget, schedule, quality, and safety requirements. This role will support proposals, project execution, site planning, technical reviews, and field service coordination for turbine, generator, and balance of plant equipment projects.

Responsibilities

- Develop field service concepts, execution plans, and cost estimates for project proposals
- Review technical requirements, customer specifications, and applicable legal or regional regulations
- Participate in site assessments and ensure required project information is available
- Support customer clarifications and field service planning during proposal and execution phases
- Prepare specifications for purchased services and supplies and evaluate bids or offers
- Identify risks and opportunities during the proposal phase and define contingency plans
- Support project startup and detail field service plans during execution
- Participate in design review meetings and provide field-related input
- Develop project-specific procedures, safety assessments, HSE documents, and quality plans
- Determine site layouts, tooling, fixtures, and infrastructure requirements
- Support project managers and site managers in executing field service scope
- Monitor quality, schedule, and cost targets and support corrective actions as needed
- Coordinate internal and external resources required for field service work
- Provide technical support and guidance to field sites

Required Qualifications

- University diploma in Engineering or equivalent industry experience
- Technical understanding of turbines, generators, and balance of plant equipment
- Ability to read and interpret blueprints, specifications, schedules, and budgets
- Project management experience
- Strong communication and interpersonal skills
- Proficiency with Microsoft Office Suite
- Ability to travel to various project sites as needed
- Ability to meet physical requirements of the role, including use of PPE, lifting up to 50 lbs., climbing ladders and stairs, working in confined spaces, and working at heights

Preferred Qualifications

- Previous experience as a site manager or supervisor on hydro turbine or generator projects
- Field service experience within industrial, power generation, hydro, or heavy equipment environments
- Experience supporting project proposals, site planning, and field execution activities

Company Benefits & Culture

Our client offers the opportunity to support technical field service projects in a hands-on, project-based environment. This role works closely with project teams, customers, suppliers, engineering, quality, purchasing, logistics, and field service leadership to ensure successful project execution from proposal through completion.

Apply through this board and our recruiting team will be in touch.

job.title: Personal Executive Assistant
job.description:
Executive Personal Assistant
Location: Must be located in Lake Mary, FL or Big Sky, Montana (Hybrid | Travel Required)
Compensation Starting At: $90,000
Contract: 3–6 Month Contract | Intent to Convert to Permanent
Reports To: Chief Executive Officer / Confidential Executive Client

We are seeking an experienced and discreet Executive Personal Assistant to support a high-profile, confidential CEO client. This unique opportunity combines executive-level administrative responsibilities with personal support in a fast-paced, high-touch environment. The ideal candidate is polished, proactive, adaptable, and thrives in dynamic settings while handling sensitive matters with absolute professionalism and discretion.

This role blends corporate-level executive assistance with personal support, event coordination, and high-level travel management. You will serve as a trusted right hand to the executive, ensuring seamless execution of both business and personal priorities. The environment is fast-moving and ever-changing, making flexibility, anticipation of needs, and strong problem-solving skills essential for success.

This is a hybrid role based in Lake Mary, FL, with travel required for executive meetings, private events, seasonal stays, and business engagements, including travel to Montana.

### Key Responsibilities

- Provide full executive and personal administrative support to a confidential CEO client in a fast-paced environment.
- Manage complex, high-volume calendars and daily priorities across multiple time zones using TimeTree or comparable scheduling systems.
- Coordinate domestic and international travel arrangements, logistics, and detailed itineraries.
- Handle personal errands, appointments, and lifestyle management tasks with the utmost discretion and professionalism.
- Coordinate logistics for meetings, corporate engagements, private functions, and high-profile events, including vendor management and on-site support.
- Draft, proofread, and manage executive correspondence, presentations, reports, and documents with exceptional attention to detail.
- Serve as a trusted liaison between the executive, internal teams, external partners, vendors, and VIP clientele.
- Maintain strict confidentiality regarding sensitive business and personal matters, including experience working under NDAs.
- Anticipate executive needs, proactively resolve issues, and ensure seamless execution of plans in a rapidly evolving environment.
- Support administrative projects, operational initiatives, and special assignments as needed.
- Maintain professionalism and composure in all interactions while representing the executive and organization positively.

### Qualifications

- 5+ years of experience as an Executive Assistant, Personal Assistant, or Executive Administrative Assistant supporting C-level leadership or high-profile clients.
- Proven experience handling confidential information with discretion and professionalism, including working under NDAs.
- Strong communication, organizational, multitasking, and time management skills.
- Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite (Outlook, Word, Excel, PowerPoint).
- Highly adaptable and comfortable working in fast-paced, evolving environments.
- Strong attention to detail with the ability to anticipate needs and problem-solve independently.
- Valid driver's license and reliable transportation for errands and local support.
- Flexible availability for occasional evenings, weekends, and travel as needed.
- Startup company experience is highly preferred.
- Preferred backgrounds include real estate, luxury services, hospitality, event coordination, corporate, or professional services environments.
- Familiarity with tools such as TimeTree, CRM systems, or project management software is a plus.

### Ideal Candidate Attributes

- Confidential & Trustworthy: Exercises discretion and integrity in handling sensitive information.
- Adaptable & Resourceful: Comfortable managing shifting priorities in fast-moving environments.
- Service-Oriented: Anticipates needs and consistently delivers high-level support.
- Professional & Polished: Maintains composure and professionalism in all settings.
- Self-Sufficient: Operates independently while maintaining excellent communication and follow-through.
- Startup Mentality: Comfortable wearing multiple hats and thriving in entrepreneurial environments.

### Additional Details

- Contract Duration: 3–6 months with strong intent to convert to permanent.
- Travel: Approximately 20–50%; all business-related travel expenses are covered.
- Schedule: Hybrid with flexibility to support executive needs.
- Interview Process: Two-step interview process including Zoom and in-person interviews for local candidates.
- Location Requirement: Candidates must currently reside in the Greater Orlando / Lake Mary, FL area or Big Sky, Montana.
- Immediate Availability: Candidates with immediate travel availability are strongly preferred due to upcoming executive events and engagements.

Apply through this board and our recruiting team will be in touch.

job.title: Operations Manager
job.description:
Location: Springfield, OR
Employment Type: Direct Hire | Full-Time
Pay Rate: $150,000 – $200,000 / year

Introduction

The client is seeking an experienced Manager of Operations to lead all aspects of their Springfield, OR production facility. This site employs approximately 40 team members who support the paper industry through mechanical services and the production of covers for paper machine rolls. The Manager of Operations will be accountable for achieving annual targets across safety, quality, efficiency, production, and profitability, while developing and executing a clear operational strategy.

Responsibilities

- Provide clear direction aligned with site strategy, goals, and commitments, with a strong emphasis on safety, quality, and financial performance
- Foster a high-performance, collaborative, and inclusive work environment
- Drive employee engagement through transparent communication on business objectives and performance
- Maintain and execute succession plans, including development actions for high-potential employees and corrective actions for performance gaps
- Initiate documented solutions to address gaps in safety, quality, or financial performance
- Ensure consistent achievement of safety, quality, production, and efficiency targets
- Identify and optimize critical aspects of the operation through data-driven decision-making
- Embed standardized systems and continuous improvement practices (Lean, OE) across the operation
- Partner with Sales and Product Management to understand and meet customer expectations for delivery and quality
- Lead improvement and capital projects at the site
- Communicate regularly with employees and leadership regarding business results, performance levels, and operational priorities
- Promote clarity of expectations, alignment to goals, and accountability for results

Required Qualifications

- Minimum of 5 years of leadership experience, including at least 1 year in operations management
- Bachelor's degree in a technical or business discipline, or equivalent experience
- Knowledge and experience with operational excellence, lean manufacturing, and continuous improvement practices
- Proficiency with MRP/ERP systems and data analytics tools (JD Edwards, Power BI, Pyramid BI)
- Ability to extract, analyze, and interpret data to support decision-making
- Proven ability to coach, mentor, and develop employees at all levels
- Strong communication skills and customer-service orientation
- Must be able to work full-time onsite at the Springfield, OR facility
- Ability to meet physical demands of an industrial environment, including standing, walking, and occasional travel

Preferred Qualifications

- Familiarity with AI and low-code technologies such as MS Copilot, Power Apps, or MS Forms

- Experience leading capital improvement projects

- Background in the paper, industrial, or manufacturing sector

Apply through this board and our recruiting team will be in touch.

job.title: Sr. Marketing Analytics Analyst
job.description:
Location: Orlando, FL (Hybrid 3 days onsite)

Pay Rate: $45-$50/hr

Employment Type: Contract (12 months, potential to convert)

Introduction

4 Corner Resources is seeking a Senior Marketing Analytics Analyst for a fast-moving digital analytics and testing team based in Orlando. The team covers digital analytics and testing across Marketing, Analytics, and business leadership.

Responsibilities

- Analyze paid and owned channel performance to surface trends, creative insights, and optimization opportunities
- Measure campaign effectiveness and ROI across digital and offline channels
- Build and maintain multi-touch attribution models and incrementality testing frameworks
- Design and execute A/B tests using statistically sound methods
- Conduct customer segmentation, funnel analysis, and lifecycle/retention analytics
- Build scalable dashboards and reporting tools
- Query and validate large datasets using SQL and cloud platforms
- Translate findings into clear recommendations for both technical and non-technical stakeholders

Requirements

- 4+ years in marketing analytics, digital analytics, or growth analytics
- Advanced SQL skills with hands-on experience at scale
- Direct experience with Google Analytics, Looker Studio, Power BI, and BigQuery
- Working knowledge of attribution models, A/B testing, and marketing measurement methodology
- Experience with customer segmentation, funnel analysis, or audience analytics
- Strong communication skills across technical and non-technical audiences

Preferred Qualifications

- R, Python, or Databricks experience
- Background in incrementality testing or advanced attribution
- Forecasting or predictive analytics applied to marketing investment
- Exposure to AI tools or automation workflows in an analytics context
- Experience with experimentation frameworks and marketing technology platforms

Apply through this board and our recruiting team will be in touch.

job.title: Unified Communications (UC) Engineer
job.description:
4 Corner Resources is looking for a hands-on UC professional to support and deliver on a complex telephony environment. You'll own day-to-day operations and active project work from day one, working independently in a fast-paced setting.

What You'll Do

- Lead telephony project implementations end-to-end, planning, configuration, installation, and go-live
- Travel to client sites to install and configure phones and UC infrastructure
- Support Cisco voice platforms including UCM, Webex Calling (Multi-Tenant and Dedicated Instance), PCCE, Unity, and CER
- Troubleshoot and resolve complex incidents, including direct vendor engagement through to close
- Contribute to disaster recovery planning, documentation, and testing
- Provide after-hours support when project timelines require it, go-lives, weekend cutovers, and post-close installations

What You'll Bring

- Deep, hands-on experience across the Cisco voice product stack, UCM, Webex Calling, PCCE, Unity, CER, and related platforms
- Proven project implementation experience, not just operational support
- Strong IP networking fundamentals (SIP trunking, routing, switching)
- A valid driver's license, reliable personal vehicle, and a clean driving record
- Ability to travel to multiple sites and ramp quickly on an active project queue
- Clear communication skills for vendor management and cross-functional coordination

Nice to Have

- Experience in healthcare or another regulated environment
- Familiarity with emerging UC and collaboration technologies

Compensation: Starting at $40/hour
Schedule: Standard business hours with occasional after-hours and weekend coverage for go-lives and cutovers
Location: 100% onsite, multi-site travel required; corporate vehicle available at times

Apply through this board and our recruiting team will be in touch.

job.title: Computer Repair Technician
job.description:
4 Corner Resources is hiring Computer Technicians to join a fast-paced technical team in Orlando. These hands-on opportunities support the repair, imaging, and preparation of computer devices in a high-volume production environment. Whether your experience is in hardware repair or software imaging, we have an opportunity for you.

### Role 1: Repair Technician

- Pay: $17.00/hr.
- Schedule: Monday-Friday, 2:30 PM to 11:00 PM
- Primary Focus: Diagnose and repair computer hardware
* Replace components as needed
* Support high-volume device repair operations
* Perform quality checks to ensure devices are functioning properly

### Role 2: Imaging/QC Technician

- Pay: $16.00/hr.
- Schedule: Monday-Friday, 6:00 AM to 2:30 PM
- Primary Focus: Software imaging, device configuration, and quality control (no hardware repairs)
* Image and configure approximately 300 laptops per shift
* Install software updates and prepare devices for deployment
* Perform final quality inspections
* Ensure devices meet shipping and configuration standards

### Qualifications (Both positions):

- Must be able to pass a Level 2 background check
- At least 6 months of experience working with computers or electronics preferred
- CompTIA A+ certification preferred
- Basic technical knowledge and troubleshooting skills
- Familiarity with Windows operating systems (Windows 8/10 and Server 2012/2016)
- Ability to lift up to 50 lbs. and sit for extended periods
- Strong written and verbal communication skills
- Comfortable working in a fast-paced environment and willing to assist with general warehouse duties as needed

Apply through this board and our recruiting team will be in touch.

job.title: Service Desk Analyst
job.description:
4 Corner Resources is hiring multiple Service Desk Analysts for one of Central Florida's largest and most respected healthcare organizations. These are 6-month contract opportunities with the potential for extension or permanent hire. This is an excellent opportunity for IT professionals looking to gain experience in an enterprise healthcare environment while building their technical support skills.

Service Desk Analyst Responsibilities:

- Serve as the first point of contact for end users seeking technical assistance
- Provide phone-based support by troubleshooting issues, answering questions, researching solutions, and documenting resolutions
- Support users with issues related to the organization's Electronic Medical Record (EMR) system
- Create, update, and manage support tickets while maintaining accurate documentation
- Escalate complex issues to the appropriate support teams when necessary
- Deliver exceptional customer service while meeting service-level expectations

Service Desk Analyst Qualifications:

- At least 1 year of technical support, help desk, or service desk experience
- Experience working with ticketing systems such as ServiceNow, Salesforce Service Cloud, Remedy, or similar platforms
- Minimum of 3 years of customer service experience
- Proficiency with Microsoft Office applications
- Strong communication, troubleshooting, and problem-solving skills
- Ability to work independently in a hybrid work environment

Service Desk Analyst Details:

- Location: Orlando, FL
- Schedule: Hybrid (1 day onsite, 4 days remote)
- Training: Fully onsite in Orlando, FL (1-4 weeks)
- Availability: Must have open availability, including nights, weekends, and rotating shifts
- Pay Rate: Up to $19.00/hour
- Duration: 6-month contract (with potential for extension or permanent placement based on performance and budget)

Apply through this board and our recruiting team will be in touch.

job.title: Automation Designer
job.description:
Location: Appleton, WI
Employment Type: Direct Hire
Pay Rate: Up to $75,000

This is an entry/junior level role on an automation engineering team responsible for the design, upgrade, and integration of controls systems on active paper production equipment. It's suited for someone with 0 to 5 years of experience in industrial controls looking to build a career in a technically engaging, drawing-intensive environment.

Responsibilities

- Create and update electrical schematics, wiring diagrams, biopanel layouts, junction box panel drawings, and interface/network drawings using CAD software
- Enter parts, materials, and documentation into SAP (ERP system)
- Coordinate with vendors and visit vendor facilities to support equipment builds and inspect completed assemblies
- Manage spare parts inventory and materials sourcing
- Support machine upgrades and enhancements on existing field equipment
- Review designs for compliance with engineering standards and customer specifications
- Collaborate with internal teams including sales, supply chain, manufacturing, and quality to maintain project schedules
- Assist in developing design standards and supporting senior automation engineers

Required Qualifications

- Background in industrial controls
- Experience with Rockwell Automation components
- Proficiency with CAD software for electrical/controls design (AutoCAD E or COMOS preferred)
- Ability to commute to or relocate to Appleton, WI

Preferred Qualifications

- Associates degree or higher in Electromechanical Design or a related field
- Experience with SAP or similar ERP systems
- Paper or pulp mill industry background
- Familiarity with pneumatic and/or hydraulic systems

Compensation and Benefits

- Up to $75,000 based on experience

- Hybrid schedule available (Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday in-office minimum per corporate policy)

- Minimal travel required; most vendors are local to the Appleton area

Apply through this board and our recruiting team will be in touch.

job.title: Strategic Account Manager
job.description:
Location: Remote | Alabama, Louisiana, Oklahoma, and Mississippi (up to 60% travel)
Pay Rate: $143,000 / salary
Employment Type: Full-Time | Exempt

Introduction
The client is looking for a results-driven Strategic Account Manager to own and grow a portfolio of key accounts in the Pulp and Paper industry. You'll serve as the primary point of contact for Products & Services, acting as a trusted advisor while identifying new business opportunities and delivering solutions that drive growth on both sides of the relationship.

Responsibilities

- Own account plans and deliver on KPIs including orders, sales, profitability, win rate, and NPS
- Serve as the primary client contact for Products & Services across your account portfolio
- Lead contract negotiations, pricing discussions, and service agreements
- Collaborate with internal technical and product teams to develop tailored client solutions
- Build and strengthen relationships from the C-suite to the plant floor
- Stay current on market trends, competitive offerings, and customer developments to differentiate the client's value

Required Qualifications

- Bachelor's degree in Business, Engineering, Chemistry, or related field
- 5+ years of account management or sales experience
- Strong understanding of Pulp and Paper manufacturing processes and market dynamics
- Exceptional communication, negotiation, and presentation skills
- Willingness to travel up to 60% across Alabama, Louisiana, Oklahoma, and Mississippi
- Proficiency with Microsoft Office Suite

Preferred Qualifications

- Experience leading cross-functional or multi-disciplinary teams

- Background in value-based selling and strategic account planning

- Familiarity with industrial or manufacturing customer environments

Apply through this board and our recruiting team will be in touch.

job.title: Inbound Customer Service Representative
job.description:
We're looking for upbeat, motivated individuals to join our Inbound Customer Service team, helping guests make the most of their vacation packages. You'll guide callers through planning and booking their trips, offering expert advice, resolving concerns, and creating a positive, memorable experience from start to finish. If you're energized by great conversations and enjoy making someone's day, this could be the perfect fit.

Inbound Customer Service Representative Available Shifts and Pay

- AM Shift | Monday – Friday | 9:00 AM – 4:00 PM | $17/hour + bonuses
- PM Shift | Monday – Friday | 3:00 PM – 10:00 PM | $19/hour + bonuses

Inbound Customer Service Representative Required Qualification:
• Friendly, outgoing personality with a customer-first mindset
• Excellent verbal communication and active listening skills
• Dependable and committed to consistent attendance
• Ability to follow structured processes and meet performance targets
• Receptive to feedback and quick to apply coaching
• Previous experience in customer service, hospitality, sales, or leadership is a plus
• Must be at least 18 years old

Inbound Customer Service Representative Key Responsibilities:
• Take incoming calls from guests ready to plan and book their vacations
• Assist with choosing destinations, selecting travel dates, and completing reservations
• Collect payments and ensure booking details are accurate and complete
• Answer questions, resolve concerns, and offer helpful travel suggestions
• Build product and destination knowledge to deliver confident, informed service
• Use strong communication to highlight value, address hesitations, and create urgency
• Bring positive energy to each interaction and exceed service expectations
• Embrace coaching and continuous improvement to support personal and team growth
• Hit performance goals and contribute to a high-achieving team environment

Apply through this board and our recruiting team will be in touch.

job.title: Inbound Customer Service Representative - 4 Day Work Week
job.description:
We're hiring upbeat, motivated individuals for our Inbound Customer Service team. This isn't your typical 9–5. With a 4-day workweek (Saturday – Tuesday), you'll enjoy longer time off while helping guests make the most of their vacation packages. You'll guide callers through planning and booking their trips, offering expert advice, resolving concerns, and creating a positive, memorable experience from start to finish. If you're energized by great conversations and love the idea of a compressed schedule (Saturday – Tuesday), this is the perfect opportunity.

What we're looking for:

• A friendly, outgoing personality with a customer-first mindset
• Strong verbal communication and active listening skills
• Dependable attendance—especially on your 4 dedicated workdays (Saturday – Tuesday)
• Ability to follow structured processes and meet performance targets
• Openness to feedback and coaching
• Experience in customer service, hospitality, sales, or leadership is a plus

What you'll do during your 4-day workweek:

• Answer inbound calls from guests ready to book their vacations
• Help choose destinations, travel dates, and finalize reservations
• Process payments and ensure booking details are accurate
• Answer questions, resolve concerns, and offer helpful travel suggestions
• Use product and destination knowledge to deliver confident, informed service
• Communicate clearly to highlight value, build urgency, and address objections
• Exceed service expectations with a positive, professional tone
• Embrace coaching and growth to keep improving
• Meet individual and team performance goals within your 4-day shift window

Schedule & pay:

- Sat–Tues, 9:00 AM – 6:00 PM
- Pay: $17/hour + performance bonuses

Apply through this board and our recruiting team will be in touch.

job.title: Outbound Call Center Representative
job.description:
Job Title: Outbound Call Center Representative
Location: Myrtle Beach, SC – Onsite
Shifts:

- AM Shift: Mon–Fri, 9:00 AM – 4:00 PM
- PM Shift: Mon–Fri, 3:00 PM – 10:00 PM

Pay Rate:

- AM Shift: $17/HR + performance bonuses
- PM Shift: $19/hour + performance bonuses

We're hiring Outbound Call Center Representatives to join our team in Myrtle Beach, SC. In this role, you'll engage with guests by phone to help them plan and book their ideal vacation experience. If you're confident, personable, and motivated by helping people create unforgettable getaways, this is an excellent opportunity to grow your career in a fun, fast-paced environment.

Required Qualifications

- Energetic, outgoing personality with a strong customer service mindset
- Excellent verbal communication and active listening skills
- Reliable, punctual, and committed to strong attendance
- Comfortable working in a structured, goal-driven environment
- Open to feedback and eager to learn and improve
- Previous experience in customer service, hospitality, or sales is a plus
- Must be 18 years of age or older

Day-to-Day Responsibilities

- Place outbound calls to guests interested in planning vacations
- Guide customers through destination options, booking dates, and reservation details
- Process payments and accurately confirm all booking information
